purpose.summary: This COBOL program is a CICS module that logs errors to a transient data queue (TDQ) named CSSL. It retrieves the current date and time, formats them, and writes an error record containing the transaction ID, program name, date, and time to the TDQ. If the error is critical, the program terminates the CICS task.
purpose.business_context: This program is part of an error handling system within a CICS environment, providing a mechanism to record and potentially react to application errors.

outputs.name: CSSL
outputs.description: Transient Data Queue (TDQ) to which error log records are written.

called_programs.purpose: Terminates the CICS task after a critical error is logged.

business_rules.description: If the error is flagged as critical, the program terminates the CICS task.
business_rules.logic_summary: Checks the ERR-CRITICAL flag. If set, performs 9990-END-ROUTINE, which in turn calls 9000-TERMINATE and issues a CICS RETURN.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph is the main error logging routine. It retrieves the current date and time using CICS ASKTIME and FORMATTIME commands (lines 4-12). It then moves the CICS transaction ID (WS-CICS-TRANID), program name (WS-PGM-AUTH), current date (WS-CUR-DATE-X6), and current time (WS-CUR-TIME-X6) into the ERROR-LOG-RECORD (lines 14-17). The ERROR-LOG-RECORD is then written to the CSSL transient data queue using a CICS WRITEQ TD command (lines 19-24). Finally, it checks if the error is critical (ERR-CRITICAL) and, if so, performs the 9990-END-ROUTINE to terminate the CICS task (lines 26-28).
This paragraph provides a standard exit point from the 9500-LOG-ERROR paragraph. It consists solely of the EXIT statement and returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph is responsible for terminating the CICS task. It first calls the 9000-TERMINATE paragraph (line 37), presumably to perform cleanup or other termination procedures. After 9000-TERMINATE completes, it issues a CICS RETURN command (lines 39-40) to end the current CICS task and return control to CICS.
This paragraph provides a standard exit point from the 9990-END-ROUTINE paragraph. It consists solely of the EXIT statement and returns control to the calling paragraph.

cics_operations.purpose: Formats the absolute time into YYMMDD and TIME formats.
Writes an error log record to the CSSL transient data queue.
